target.title: fix(agents): rename misleading system prompt section to Time Zone
target.body:
## Behavior

The system prompt section for user timezone information is titled `## Current Date & Time`, but it only contains the timezone string (e.g. `Time zone: America/Chicago`). The actual current date and time are intentionally omitted for prompt cache stability — this is documented and correct behavior.

However, the misleading title creates a contradiction that increases the chance of temporal reasoning errors by the model. When a user asks about a milestone being due "today" or "yesterday", the model may incorrectly infer dates because the section heading implies a full timestamp is available when it is not.

This was reported in #63181 with a concrete example: an agent described an April 8 milestone as "due yesterday" on April 8 itself, likely because the `## Current Date & Time` heading created a false affordance.

## Environment

- **OS**: Linux 6.8.0-71-generic
- **Node**: v24.14.1
- **OpenClaw commit**: HEAD of `fix/misleading-prompt-section-63181`
- **Test command**: `npx vitest run src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- **Repository**: openclaw/openclaw

## Steps

1. Check the current system prompt heading on main branch:
   ```bash
   git checkout main
   grep -n "Current Date & Time" src/agents/system-prompt.ts
   # Line 390: return ["## Current Date & Time", `Time zone: ${params.userTimezone}`, ""];
   ```

2. Verify the fix on the PR branch:
   ```bash
   git checkout fix/misleading-prompt-section-63181
   grep -n "Time Zone" src/agents/system-prompt.ts
   # Line 390: return ["## Time Zone", `Time zone: ${params.userTimezone}`, ""];
   ```

3. Run the regression test:
   ```bash
   npx vitest run src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
   ```

4. Verify docs alignment (no stale references remain):
   ```bash
   rg "Current Date & Time" docs/ src/agents/
   # Should return no results except historical comments
   ```

## Evidence

### Before the fix
The system prompt contained a section with a misleading title:
```text
## Current Date & Time
Time zone: America/Chicago
```

### After the fix
The section title now accurately reflects the content:
```text
## Time Zone
Time zone: America/Chicago
```

### Test assertion updated
```diff
- expect(prompt, testCase.name).toContain("## Current Date & Time");
+ expect(prompt, testCase.name).toContain("## Time Zone");
```

### Test results
```bash
$ npx vitest run src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       15 passed (15)
Duration    2.45s
```

### Docs alignment
Updated 3 docs files to use the new section name:
- `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md` — 3 references updated
- `docs/concepts/timezone.md` — 1 reference updated
- `docs/date-time.md` — 1 reference updated

## Observed result

- The `buildTimeSection` function now returns `## Time Zone` instead of `## Current Date & Time`
- All 15 system-prompt tests pass (1 file, 15 tests)
- The change is isolated to the heading string; prompt caching, section ordering, and other behavior are unaffected
- The misleading temporal affordance is eliminated
- Docs are now aligned with the code change
- No stale references to `Current Date & Time` remain in user-facing docs

## Not tested

- No live model run was performed to verify that temporal reasoning errors are fully eliminated (the fix is structural, not behavioral)
- No integration tests with downstream agents consuming the prompt
- No manual verification of the rendered prompt in a real OpenClaw session (only unit-test coverage)
- The docs changes were verified with `rg` search, not built/rendered

---

## Fix summary

**Renames the system prompt timezone section from `## Current Date & Time` to `## Time Zone` to eliminate the misleading affordance that causes temporal reasoning errors.**

### Files changed
- `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` — rename heading in `buildTimeSection()`
- `src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts` — sync test assertion
- `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md` — update 3 references to new section name
- `docs/concepts/timezone.md` — update 1 reference
- `docs/date-time.md` — update 1 reference

This is a minimal, non-breaking change: the section still contains only the timezone, and the existing "use `session_status` for current date/time" guidance remains unchanged.

## Related
- Fixes #63181
- Related to #38782 (temporal grounding in startup greetings)
- Related to #63071 (prompt cache stability vs timestamp injection)
target.author: xuzehan-icenter
